expectations.text: Title uses Conventional Commits style (e.g., feat(booking): ...) since repo history is conventional
expectations.evidence: Title begins 'feat(booking): require explicit session booking before message send' — Conventional Commits scoped form.
Title is 77 characters (including the trailing '[MED-481]' tag), exceeding the 72-character limit.

expectations.text: Body has a Summary section (or equivalent lead paragraph) describing what the PR does in product/system-level language, not file-level
expectations.evidence: '## Summary' describes the behavior change (explicit booking step, preventing double-booking) at the system level without enumerating files.

expectations.text: Body has a Why or motivation section explaining the race-condition problem the change addresses
expectations.evidence: Summary states the old flow 'made it impossible to prevent double-booking of the same slot', and 'Why this shape' explains design choices. The race/double-booking motivation is present.

expectations.text: MED-481 issue link appears in the body (e.g., 'Closes MED-481', 'Refs: MED-481', or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'Ticket: MED-481' appears in the Summary section.

expectations.text: Body contains a bulleted list of key changes (load-bearing changes, not file-by-file)
expectations.evidence: The 'Changes' section is explicitly organized file-by-file (bullets headed 'New src/booking/lock.py', 'New src/booking/service.py', 'src/messages.py', 'Test') — the notes even confirm 'Changes is grouped by file/module and matches the three commits one-to-one'.

expectations.text: Body acknowledges the behavioral change for missing-session requests (409 / breaking change risk)
expectations.evidence: Changes bullet for messages.py: 'returns {"error": "no_session", "status": 409} when the caller did not book first. The legacy send (implicit-create) is kept untouched for backwards compatibility.'

expectations.text: Body has a Review notes / review-instructions section telling reviewers where to start (file path) and what to scrutinize
expectations.evidence: No 'Review notes' / 'Where to start' section exists. The body has Summary, Changes, Why this shape, Test plan, and Follow-ups — none direct reviewers to a starting file or what to scrutinize.

expectations.text: Body does NOT enumerate the individual commits (no 'Commits:' list, no per-commit headers, no commit hashes)
expectations.evidence: Body contains no commit hashes, no 'Commits:' list, and no per-commit headers (despite notes mentioning the file groupings mirror commits, the body itself does not enumerate them).

expectations.text: Body does NOT contain tooling references ('Claude', 'AI-written', 'verified by running tests', test-output dumps, etc.)
expectations.evidence: No tooling mentions. Test plan lists pytest invocations as plan items (not output dumps), which is standard PR checklist content.

expectations.text: Body length is between 15 and 60 lines (sensible feature-PR size)
expectations.evidence: Body is 55 lines, within the 15-60 line range.
